headline: A detached-backed BufferSource now fails closed with a typed error at every byte-input boundary.
summary: A TypedArray or ArrayBuffer whose backing store has been transferred or structuredClone'd away is detached: it reads as zero-length. The toolkit's input boundaries coerced such a value with a Buffer.isBuffer fast-path that returned it as-is, so the empty view flowed downstream -- and pki.webcrypto digest / sign / verify silently hashed or signed EMPTY input (a fail-open) rather than rejecting it, while other paths surfaced a raw TypeError or a misleading truncated-input verdict. Every byte-input boundary -- pki.asn1.decode, pki.cbor.decode, pki.ct.parseSctList, and the pki.webcrypto digest / sign / verify / getRandomValues input coercion -- now re-views the input through Buffer.from(view.buffer, view.byteOffset, view.byteLength) and rejects a detached backing store with the module's typed error (asn1/not-buffer, cbor/not-buffer, ct/bad-input, webcrypto/data), threading the underlying failure as the error cause. A caller catches it with a single instanceof pki.errors.PkiError check. No API surface changes; hashing stays synchronous and no path is newly network-touching.

Fixed: pki.webcrypto digest / sign / verify no longer silently process a detached-backed Buffer as EMPTY input (a fail-open where a transferred backing ArrayBuffer left the view zero-length); a detached BufferSource is now rejected with a typed webcrypto/data error, as is getRandomValues.

pki.asn1.decode, pki.cbor.decode, and pki.ct.parseSctList reject a detached-backed Buffer or view with a typed error (asn1/not-buffer, cbor/not-buffer, ct/bad-input) instead of a raw TypeError or a misleading truncated-input verdict. The underlying byte-view failure is threaded as the error cause.
